records.ety: [OE. laddre, AS. hlǣder, hlǣdder; akin to OFries. hladder, OHG. leitara, G. leiter, and from the root of E. lean, v. √40. See Lean, v. i., and cf. Climax.]

defs: A frame usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened cross strips or rounds forming steps.
quotes: Some the engines play, And some, more bold, mount ladders to the fire.

defs: That which resembles a ladder in form or use;
that by means of which one attains to eminence; as, to climb the corporate ladder.
